{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Gunderam Defence| |rd|nd|bd| |kd|bd|nd|rd|pd|pd|pd|pd|qd|pd|pd|pd| | | | | | | | | | | | |pd| | | | | | | |pl| | | | | | | | |nl| | |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl||rl|| }} = Gunderam Defence = Also known as the '''Brazilian Defense''' or the '''Câmara Defense'''. This move attempts to defend Black's e-pawn, but in doing so it shuts the king's bishop in. ===2... Qe7=== The idea for Black is to transpose into a [[Chess/King's Indian Defence|King's Indian Defence]] [''source needed''], but with forceful play this option is not entirely possible. Black could also play 3...c6,4...d6,5...Bg4 or 5...h6 and develop slowly but surely. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Qe7'''
3 4
[[/3. Bc4|Bc4]]
c6		 O-O
d6		 +=
